headline: Mental health detainees held 280 days average in institutions
context: Persons detained under the Mental Health Act 2001 for assessment spent an average of 280 days in psychiatric institutions before case resolution.

cards.big: Mental health detainees spend average 280 days in institutions before resolution
cards.sub: Involuntary detention under Mental Health Act 2001 lacks adequate safeguards

cards.big: Mental Health Act 2001 Section 10 allows involuntary detention for assessment
cards.sub: Review mechanism requires medical opinion but not independent judicial oversight

cards.big: CRPD Article 14 requires liberty and security protections for persons with disabilities
cards.sub: Indefinite psychiatric detention without consent raises serious rights concerns

cards.big: Malaysia has only 410 psychiatrists serving 32 million population as of 2025
cards.sub: Staffing shortages contribute to prolonged detention and delayed assessments

cards.big: 280 days of detention for assessment is warehousing, not treatment
Health Ministry proposed Mental Health Act review committee in January 2025
